title: Rotational generator
abstract: The invention relates to a rotational generator for generating electricity from the rotational energy of a moving vehicle, the rational generator includes a rotor which is defined by a plurality of magnets which are securable to a rim such that the plurality of magnets rotate coincident with the rim. The rotational generator also includes a stator in the form of an annular disk which is securable to a wheel hub. The stator is shaped and dimensioned to fit inside the rim and includes a plurality of coil windings which extends radially outward toward the periphery of the annular disk. In use, when the rotational generator is installed on a vehicle the plurality of magnets will rotate coincident the rotation of the wheel while the stator is retained in a stationary position thereby inducing an electrical current in the coil windings.
claims:
Claims 1. A rotational generator for generating electricity by utilising the rotational energy of a moving vehicle with a dual wheel system defined by an outer and an inner rim, the rational generator includes; a rotor which includes a plurality of magnets, the rotor attached to the outer rim of the vehicle allowing the magnets to rotate via the coincident rotation of the wheel; and a stator positioned on an operative outside of the outer rim, the stator consisting of a plurality of coils which in use overlap with the plurality of magnets, the stator retained in a stationary condition with respect to the rotational movement of the rotor, the rotation of the magnets relative the stationary stator resulting in an electrical current induced in the plurality of coils. 2. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 1 , in which the rotor is shaped and dimensioned to be received by an inner portion of the outer rim of the vehicle. 3. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 2, in which the rotor is in the form of a base member with plurality of magnets secured to a plurality of fingers which extend perpendicular from the outer edge of the base member towards an operatively outer side of the rim. 4. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 3, in which the rotor includes a plurality of mounting means for mounting the rotor onto the vehicleâs wheel to rotate coincident with the rotation of the wheel. 5. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 4, in which the plurality of mounting means are in the form of apertures which are positioned to correspond with wheel studs which extend from a hub of the wheel. 6. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 1 , in which the stator is in the form of a circular base plate with a plurality of members which extend form an inner face of the base plate towards an operatively inner side of the rim. 7. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 6, in which coil windings are secured to the plurality of members which extend from the face of the base plate. 8. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 1 , in which the stator is connected to the wheel hub through a bearing assembly to constrain the movement of the stator relative the rotating wheel. 9. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 8, in which the bearing assembly includes an electrical contact plug through which an electrical cable is placed and is in contact with the plurality of coils. 10. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 9, in which the stator includes a stabilizer arm which is connected to the stator and a frame of the vehicle. 11. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 10, in which the stabilizer arm includes a compression zone capable of being compressed and extended through the vertical movement of the stator. 12. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 10, in which the stabilizer arm includes an electrical passage through which the electrical cable runs from the electrical plug towards a battery. 13. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 9, in which the rotor is installable onto the vehicle hub and the stator is installable on the bearing assembly so that the coil windings overlap the plurality of magnets and the plurality of magnets rotate around the coil windings. 14. A rotational generator for generating electricity from the rotational energy of a driveshaft, the rational generator includes; a rotor defined by a plurality of magnets which are attachable to the driveshaft, the plurality of magnets capable of rotating coincident together with the rotation of the driveshaft; and a stator consisting of a plurality of coils, the stator placed around the driveshaft where the rotor is positioned, the stator includes at least one bearing placed between the stator and the driveshaft allowing the stator to be retained in a stationary condition relative 

 the rotational movement of the driveshaft, the rotation of the plurality of magnets inside the plurality of coils resulting in an electrical current being induced in the plurality of coils. 15. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 14, in which the stator is in the form of a casing dimensioned to be placed around the driveshaft with the plurality of coils spaced along the length of the casing. 16. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 15, in which the casing is in the form of two semi-circular sections with fastening brackets on each section to allow the two semi-circular sections to be secured to each other around the driveshaft. 17. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 15, in which the casing includes a depression on each end in which a split bearing is placed to allow the driveshaft to rotate independently of the stator. 18. A rotational generator for generating electricity from the rotational energy of a moving vehicle, the rational generator includes; a rotor which is defined by a plurality of magnets, the plurality of magnets positioned proximate an operatively inner surface of a rim in order to rotate coincident with the rim; and a stator consisting of an annular disk securable to a wheel hub, the stator is shaped and dimensioned to fit inside the wheel and includes a plurality of coils which extends radially outward toward the periphery of the annular disk, the stator retained in a stationary condition, by being secured to the wheel hub, with respect to the rotational movement of the rotor. 19. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 18, in which the plurality of magnets are secured to an operatively inner surface along the width of the rim. 20. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 19, in which the rotational generator includes a first and a second dust cover, the first and second dust cover receivable inside the rim, on opposed sides of the stator to prevent ingress of dirt into components of the stator. 21. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 20, in which the first dust cover is shaped to fit inside the rim proximate a hub plate of the rim, the first dust cover securable to the rim via securing apertures which correspond with wheel studs which extend from the wheel hub. 22. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 18, in which a first dust cover in the form of a shell which is shaped to fit inside the rim. 23. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 21 , in which a first dust cover is securable to the wheel via securing apertures which correspond with wheel studs which extend from the hub of the wheel. 24. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 22, in which the plurality of magnets are secured to the inner face of the first dust cover and capable of rotating with the wheel. 25. A rotational generator for generating electricity from the rotational energy of a rail bound vehicle, the rational generator includes; a rotor which includes a plurality of magnets, the rotor is in the form of a segmented annular disk placed around an axle of the vehicle and securable to the axle allowing the magnets rotate via the coincident rotation of the axle; a stator consisting of a plurality of coils, the stator is in the form of a segmented annular disk with a bearing secured to the segmented disk and placed around the axle of the vehicle. 26. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 25, in which the plurality of magnets are secured on members extending perpendicular away from the rotor. 27. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 25, in which the plurality of coils are secured on members extending perpendicular away from the stator in order to overlap with the plurality of magnets. 28. A rotational generator as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, in which the rotational generator is used on a propeller, having a plurality of propeller blades, 

 and a propeller casing with an inner face of the propeller casing attached to the periphery of the propellerâs blades. 29. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 28, in which the propeller casing includes a plurality of magnetic strips attached to an outer face of the casing. 30. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 28, in which the propeller includes a cover with a plurality of coil winding attached to an inner face of the cover, the propeller cover placed around the propeller casing in such a manner that the outer face of the propeller casing and the inner face of the propeller cover is proximate to one another such that the rotation of the propeller blades causes the magnetic strips located on the propeller casing to rotate and thereby induce an electrical current in the coil windings located on the stationary propeller cover. 31. A rotational generator as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, in which the rotational generator is attached to a rotational element through a shaft and a gear mechanism. 32. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 31 , in which the rotational generator includes a rotor which is secured to the shaft to allow the rotor to rotate with the shaft. 33. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 31 , in which the rotor is in the form of a rim with a plurality of magnets attached to an outside of the rim. 34. The rotational generator as claimed in claim 31 , in which the rotational generator includes a stator which is in the form of a cover with a plurality of coil windings attached to an inner face of the cover, the cover shaped and dimensioned to be placed over the rotor. 35. A rotational generator for generating electricity by utilising the rotational energy of a moving vehicle with a dual wheel system defined by an outer and an inner rim as claimed in claim 1 , substantially as herein described and illustrated. 36. A rotational generator for generating electricity from the rotational energy of a rotating driveshaft as claimed in claim 14, substantially as herein described and illustrated. 37. A rotational generator for generating electricity from the rotational energy of a moving vehicle as claimed in claim 18, substantially as herein described and illustrated. 38. A rotational generator for generating electricity from the rotational energy of a rail bound vehicle as claimed in claim 25, substantially as herein described and illustrated.
